title: Concavity intro
topic_path: Determining concavity of intervals and finding points of inflection: graphical

assessment_items.question_content:
Function $f$ is graphed.

![Function f is graphed. The x-axis goes from negative 5 to 5. The graph is an M-shaped curve. The curve starts in quadrant 3, moves upward concave down to about (negative 3.5, 30), moves downward concave down to about (negative 2.5, 5), continues downward concave up to about (negative 1, negative 20), moves upward concave up to about (1, 45), continues upward concave down to about (2.5, 90), moves downward concave down, and ends in quadrant 4.](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/c4bd16ce55f9709badd9a460ebc17c2c87aa66ba)

**Select all the intervals where $f'(x)>0$ and $f''(x)>0$.**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
$f'(x)>0$ means $f$ is **increasing**.

$f'(x)<0$ means $f$ is **decreasing**.

$f''(x)>0$ means the slope of $f$ is increasing ($f$ is **concave up**).

$f''(x)<0$ means the slope of $f$ is decreasing ($f$ is **concave down**).

| | $f'(x)>0$ | $f'(x)<0$
:- | :- | :-: | :-:
| | $f$ is **increasing** | $f$ is **decreasing**
$f''(x)>0$ | $f$ is **concave up** |  ![A curve moves upward with increasing steepness.](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/fad1dc95cd761b6e7f4c581c7093841fcf16029a) | ![A curve moves downward with decreasing steepness.](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/88e8d1ff71cbe909ea256814c06e1ff2df39bd5e)
$f''(x)<0$ | $f$ is **concave down** | ![A curve moves upward with decreasing steepness.](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/f9425dbee4d4f73f6188a62f88f637018843d7c8) | ![A curve moves downward with increasing steepness.](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/4d64d76d6423a90abf605dfe2b856e7327f92a0c)

We are asked to find an interval where $f'(x)>0$ and $f''(x)>0$, so we need an interval where the graph of $f$ has this general shape:

 ![A curve moves upward with increasing steepness.](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/fad1dc95cd761b6e7f4c581c7093841fcf16029a)

Visually, this is where $f'(x)>0$ and $f''(x)>0$.

![The graph of function f has a portion of the curve highlighted. The portion moves upward concave up, from about (negative 1, negative 17) to about (0.5, 17).](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/21151ab2ab463c98a17bfe43a3e121d83248e5c0)

Now we can check each option:

Interval | Shape | Is $f'(x)>0$ and $f''(x)>0$?
:-: | :-: | :-:
$-3.5<x<-2.5$ | ![A curve moves downward with decreasing steepness.](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/1423ac842506af6991b8e635c34a9d36faf33368) | $\redD{\text{No}}$. $f''(x)>0$ but $f'(x)<0$.
$-1<x<0$ | ![A curve moves upward with increasing steepness.](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/842593fcfd6a52a51cd66950f88ff7cd344d7af3) | $\greenD{\text{Yes!}}$
$1.5<x<2.5$ | ![A curve moves upward with decreasing steepness.](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/422cf5924dbecfc98054d87696663b0145ce07e6) | $\redD{\text{No}}$. $f'(x)>0$ but $f''(x)<0$.
